explanationTemplates: They explicitly asked you out and handled logistics. The only delusion here is opening this app to overthink a date with a reservation.
This is not a clue hunt; it is a plan. They said dinner, picked Friday, and booked the table.
nextMoveTemplates: Go to dinner. Leave the conspiracy board at home.
Respond normally and show up on Friday.

explanationTemplates: They asked. You answered. This is not a mystery novel; it is a relationship.
A direct "be my girlfriend" question is not a signal to analyze. It is a conclusion.
nextMoveTemplates: Stop investigating the confirmation and enjoy being official.
Treat the relationship label like evidence because it is evidence.

explanationTemplates: They remembered your birthday and suggested a weekend plan. That is a real invite, not a secret code.
Celebrate this weekend is loose, but it is still more than a crumb.
nextMoveTemplates: Take the birthday invite at face value and make the plan concrete.
Respond normally and see what time they suggest.

explanationTemplates: A 2am "I miss u" is emotional confetti. The missing reply afterward is the part you should actually read.
Late-night feelings plus daytime silence is not stability; it is a push notification with commitment issues.
nextMoveTemplates: Wait for consistent daytime effort before treating this like progress.
Do not let one late-night text outrank the silence after it.

explanationTemplates: A stale like is not romance. It is digital archaeology, especially when they are watching stories without DMing.
Watching every story without DMing is surveillance-adjacent, not a plan.
nextMoveTemplates: Wait for an actual message before upgrading this from app noise.
Do not turn deep scrolling into direct interest without a DM.

explanationTemplates: You are surviving on digital crumbs. A story like from three weeks ago with zero texts is a thumb slip, not romance.
This is not a slow burn; it is an Instagram fossil. No texts after matching means digital crumbs, not romance.
nextMoveTemplates: Stop feeding the narrative until they use actual words.
Do not escalate a thumb movement into a relationship plot.

explanationTemplates: "Maybe sometime" plus no follow-up is not a scheduling issue. It is a polite disappearing act asking you to let this conversation die peacefully.
That "maybe sometime" was not a plan; it was a polite smoke bomb asking you to let this die peacefully.
nextMoveTemplates: Let it sit. If they want to hang out, they can produce a date like an adult.
Do not chase a maybe that already left the room.

explanationTemplates: A crying emoji after ghosting is not a comeback tour. It is a breadcrumb wearing streetwear.
Ghosting plus a selfie reaction is not them spinning the block; it is low-effort re-entry with better slang.
nextMoveTemplates: Require actual words and actual plans before reopening the door.
Do not treat a story reaction like a formal apology tour.

explanationTemplates: A fire emoji after ghosting you is not a romantic revival; it is a low-effort check to see if the door is still unlocked.
Ghosting followed by an IG story reply is not romance rising from the grave. It is a fire emoji checking whether access still works.
nextMoveTemplates: Do not reopen the door for a reaction emoji.
If they want a revival, they can use full sentences and actual effort.

explanationTemplates: A dry reply does not erase a dinner plan. They said they want to see you again and picked a day; your panic is doing unpaid overtime.
This is a plan with one dry-text speed bump, not a rejection. Saturday dinner beats today's texting tone.
nextMoveTemplates: Keep the plan and stop grading every text for emotional moisture.
Let the dinner plan matter more than one dry reply.

explanationTemplates: A room going quiet for one second is not a congressional hearing about your vibe.
You got one awkward room beat and turned it into a documentary about yourself.
nextMoveTemplates: Let one second be one second. Talk to someone before writing the verdict.
Do not make party silence carry a whole personality review.

explanationTemplates: A smiley face next to a project check-in is office seasoning, not a confession.
Morning project messages plus smiley faces is workplace rhythm with garnish, not hidden romance.
nextMoveTemplates: Answer the project question. Save the romance read for behavior outside the task list.
Look for interest that is not attached to your deliverables.

explanationTemplates: They asked for your number, followed up, and suggested a time. That is not just politeness; that is logistics.
This is not vague event politeness. They got your number, texted the next morning, and put coffee on the calendar.
nextMoveTemplates: Say yes or no like a normal person. The follow-up is real.
Treat the coffee invite as evidence, not a trap.

explanationTemplates: Sharing air near a leg press is not a signal. Until words happen, this is gym geography.
Using the machine next to yours is not courtship; it is floor-plan logistics with sweat towels.
nextMoveTemplates: Wait for words before upgrading proximity into meaning.
Treat nearby equipment as nearby equipment until there is an actual interaction.

explanationTemplates: If you always start the conversation and they never ask anything back, that is not a mystery. That is an effort imbalance.
Nice replies are not the same as mutual friendship labor. Right now you are carrying the conversation couch alone.
nextMoveTemplates: Pull back once and see if they cross the bridge without you building it.
Stop initiating for a beat and watch whether they create any momentum.

explanationTemplates: They gave you the terms and conditions out loud. Stop trying to find loopholes in the fine print.
They stated the boundary out loud. Intimacy can be confusing, but it is not a loophole in the fine print.
nextMoveTemplates: Believe the disclaimer before the cute behavior.
Do not litigate against a boundary they already stated.

explanationTemplates: This is more grounded than anxious. A slow reply does not erase a planned next date.
They planned, followed up, and gave clear positive language. Six hours is a delay, not a disappearance.
nextMoveTemplates: Treat the plan like real evidence and stop prosecuting the reply time.
Let the next date carry more weight than one slow text.

explanationTemplates: Work context means do not sprint, but dinner after work one-on-one is more than coffee near the printer.
Coworker context adds caution, but asking for dinner after work just the two of you is an actual signal.
nextMoveTemplates: Keep it normal, meet outside work, and watch whether the effort stays direct.
Treat it as a cautious signal, not an HR emergency.

explanationTemplates: They apologized, explained the delay, and offered a plan. That is repair, not abandonment.
This is not crumbs. They gave context, apologized, and asked to hang out this weekend.
nextMoveTemplates: Take the repair at face value and make the plan.
Respond to the actual follow-up instead of retrying the panic spiral.

explanationTemplates: You are operating as a free delivery driver and ATM, not a romantic prospect. Do not confuse financial investment with a relationship.
Months of traveling and paying for things without commitment makes you a free delivery driver and ATM, not a relationship.
nextMoveTemplates: Stop investing like a partner when they will not define you like one.
Require commitment before you keep funding the commute and the takeout.

explanationTemplates: A disclaimer like that is an afterthought, not an invitation. They are holding the door open because you were standing there, not because they sent for you.
A disclaimer like that is an afterthought, not an invitation. They did not send for you; they shrugged near an open door.
nextMoveTemplates: Go only if you genuinely want drinks, not because you found a hidden signal.
Treat the invite as optional because they literally did.

explanationTemplates: You were not on a date; you were a captive audience for a financial podcast. The $300 dinner was just the cover charge for crypto monologue night.
A paid dinner helps, but not when they forget you are a person and turn the table into a crypto seminar.
nextMoveTemplates: Value curiosity more than the bill.
Do not confuse expensive food with emotional availability.

explanationTemplates: A BeReal reaction does not legally cancel out 72 hours of complete silence. We are not back.
Being left on delivered for 3 days and then getting a BeReal like is not a comeback tour. We are not back.
nextMoveTemplates: Wait for an actual message, not app confetti.
Do not let one tap erase three days of silence.

explanationTemplates: Being left on read for a week and then getting an Instagram post like is a crumb, not romance.
An Instagram post like does not erase a whole week on read. That is app confetti over a silence problem.
nextMoveTemplates: Wait for actual words, not a like.
Do not let one tap rewrite a week of silence.

explanationTemplates: That is the company's corporate card, not a courtship. Drink the free iced latte and immediately stop flirting with your boss.
That is the company's corporate card, not a courtship. Workplace lunch is not forbidden romance; HR can smell this from the hallway.
nextMoveTemplates: Enjoy the coffee and keep the boundary aggressively normal.
Do not turn a 1-on-1 into an HR training module.

explanationTemplates: You are trying to build a legal case out of someone accidentally looking in your general direction.
Eye contact in a meeting is not a legal case; it is someone looking in your general direction with office lighting.
nextMoveTemplates: Get an actual interaction before opening arguments.
Treat this as visual noise until words happen.

explanationTemplates: Terminal Main Character Syndrome. Unless you were on fire, a room full of people was not secretly plotting to be your best friend.
Main Character math is undefeated and unwell. People looking around at a party does not mean they all want to be your best friend.
nextMoveTemplates: Talk to one actual person before assigning the room a group motive.
Retire the audience theory and look for a real invitation.

explanationTemplates: They check in because their bonus depends on your work. It is called project management, not a meet-cute.
Morning project check-ins are office plumbing. Useful, repetitive, and not secretly romantic.
nextMoveTemplates: Answer the project question and keep the subplot out of the status meeting.
Treat this as work rhythm unless they show interest outside the job.

explanationTemplates: You are their free emotional support chatbot. If they have time to text daily, they have time for coffee. They just don't want to.
Daily texting sounds cute until every real-life plan hits the work excuse wall.
nextMoveTemplates: Ask once for a concrete plan. If they dodge again, close the help desk.
Stop rewarding unlimited texting with unlimited patience.

explanationTemplates: It is a TikTok, not a court summons. Your best friend has the right to ignore your reels for 48 hours. Touch grass.
A best friend can leave a TikTok unread without resigning from the friendship. The reel is not legally binding.
nextMoveTemplates: Send one normal message later if needed. Do not hold a friendship trial over a video.
Let the TikTok remain unserious.

explanationTemplates: They do not have a crush on you; they have a crush on your Golden Retriever. You are strictly the middleman to the animal.
Remembering the dog's birthday is adorable, but the 14-hour reply says you may be the dog's assistant, not the lead.
nextMoveTemplates: Let the dog enjoy the attention. You still need human-level follow-through.
Do not confuse pet diplomacy with a confirmed crush.

explanationTemplates: You were not exclusively chosen; you are a warm body required to make the room look full. Go for the free cake, but this was not a targeted strike.
An invite to the entire office is crowd control with frosting. You are a warm body with access to free cake, not a targeted strike.
nextMoveTemplates: Go if it sounds fun, but do not treat a mass invite like a secret message.
Eat the cake. Retire the exclusivity theory.

86-100: Wait for real action before taking this seriously.
Do not escalate based on crumbs alone.
Pause the narrative and look for something concrete.
Stop filling in blanks they have not filled in themselves.
71-85: Look for follow-through, not just vibes.
Get clearer evidence before deciding what this means.
Do not judge this from the best moment. Watch the full pattern.
Keep the read provisional until action catches up.
41-70: Stay neutral and watch the pattern.
Give it a little time and see what repeats.
Keep things light until the situation becomes less weird.
Pay attention to whether effort increases, decreases, or stays vague.
21-40: Proceed naturally, but keep expectations realistic.
Respond and see whether they match your energy.
Keep it simple and see whether the effort continues.
The evidence is decent, so do not overwork it.
0-20: This is clear enough. Act normal.
The evidence is stronger than usual here.
Drop the detective work and respond like a person.
Treat this like a real signal unless the pattern changes.

no_concrete_followup: Wait for an actual plan, not a vague maybe.
Without follow-up, the case stays weaker than it sounds.
mixed_signals: Do not judge this from the highs only. Watch the full pattern.
Mixed signals mean the average matters more than the best moments.
direct_action: Concrete action matters more than hints, and this counts as action.
When someone makes a real move, you do not need to over-interpret.
booked_logistics: The logistics are handled. Stop trying to find a trapdoor under the reservation.
A booked plan is actual evidence. Behave accordingly.
single_low_signal: One tiny social signal is not enough to carry a full theory.
Treat this as a breadcrumb, not a conclusion.
vague_language: Vague wording is not the same as clear intent.
Do not convert a maybe into a promise.
social_media_overread: Online signals can look louder than they really are.
Do not let app behavior outweigh real-world effort.
consistent_effort: Repeated effort matters, but only if it points somewhere real.
Consistency helps, but it still needs context.
delayed_reply: A delayed reply by itself does not justify a courtroom drama.
Timing matters, but do not build the whole case on it.

high: The evidence is thin and the confidence is doing push-ups.
This is mostly vibes wearing a fake mustache as evidence.
The theory is louder than the behavior supporting it.
There are crumbs here, but you are trying to bake a wedding cake.
mid: There is something here, but not enough to declare a whole narrative.
This is not baseless, but it is also not sturdy enough to lean on.
The case has a pulse, just not a birth certificate.
A few signals exist, but the pattern is still wearing a disguise.
low: This looks more grounded than delusional.
There is actual effort here, so the panic can take a chair.
The evidence is concrete enough that you can stop interrogating it.
This is one of the clearer cases. Please do not make it weird.
